title: All about the mind rape
wikitext:
[]: All about the mind rape!             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Feb 10, 2009 5:43 am What did you all think of all the mind rape scenes in Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Feb 10, 2009 6:08 am I found the mind rape scenes to be among the most enjoyable in the series. Sitting there and watching my favorite characters screaming for help that would not come in time was refreshing.

[]: Re: All about the mind rape!             Quote Post by Another Asuka » Tue Feb 10, 2009 6:47 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: What did you all think of all the mind rape scenes in Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 10, 2009 8:15 am So, why DOES everyone call it "mind RAPE"? Are Arael's powers of instant "whether you like it or not" psychiatry like a giant penis, and Asuka's mind and soul one big vulvo-vaginal complex?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Feb 10, 2009 10:09 am I'm not sure it technically qualifies as "rape" because Asuka totally wanted it.

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Tue Feb 10, 2009 10:42 am Reichu wrote: So, why DOES everyone call it "mind RAPE"? Are Arael's powers of instant "whether you like it or not" psychiatry like a giant penis, and Asuka's mind and soul one big vulval-vaginal complex?

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:01 pm Well, it's a total mindfuck, and a forced and unsolicited one. Forced violating mindfuck = mindrape.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:26 pm I'm all for the mind-rape... Reichu wrote: So, why DOES everyone call it "mind RAPE"? Are Arael's powers of instant "whether you like it or not" psychiatry like a giant penis, and Asuka's mind and soul one big vulvo-vaginal complex?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:41 pm master_lloyd wrote: Well she has her mind invaded and assaulted without consent. So it does bring to mind rape.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Feb 10, 2009 12:52 pm Tentacle monsters be damned! I care not for your hentai stereotypes!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 10, 2009 8:27 pm Reichu wrote: So, why DOES everyone call it "mind RAPE"? Are Arael's powers of instant "whether you like it or not" psychiatry like a giant penis, and Asuka's mind and soul one big vulvo-vaginal complex?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Tue Feb 10, 2009 9:13 pm The mindrape MADE Evangelion for me, mmmkay?

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Feb 10, 2009 9:34 pm I dunno WTF happened, but it was awesome (and really late and close to finals so I was sorta thinking about my homework at the same time).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 10, 2009 10:35 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I take it your being tongue-in-cheek

[]:             Quote Post by Xeroko » Wed Feb 11, 2009 12:31 am Those sequences still make me nervous to be quite honest (especially the 22' ones).

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Wed Feb 11, 2009 3:14 am Do you mean the d.c. mindrape or the original? The original meant nothing to me and I didn't find it particularly traumatic. But the d.c. made me feel sick and vulnerable. I had to watch the episode five times in a row in order to move on. While I was watching it I was writing about the episode at the same time, so I should have been watching it as an outsider. Instead, I was sucked in to the setting and I was affected along with Asuka... Concerning the scene itself, GAINAX really outdid themselves. The symbols, the sounds, the colors, and the fantastic voice acting added up and really fleshed out Asuka's character in a meaningful way. In English the whole scene sounds forced and fake, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Xeroko » Wed Feb 11, 2009 9:09 am I meant the DC one, I have never actually seen the non DC versions of 21-24.

[]:             Quote Post by Psychodrama » Thu Feb 12, 2009 10:40 pm CorporalChaos wrote: I found the mind rape scenes to be among the most enjoyable in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 14, 2009 9:09 am I still want to know which scenes ThePRPD is actually referring to here.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Feb 14, 2009 10:50 am Reichu wrote: I still want to know which scenes ThePRPD is actually referring to here.

 and vulnerable. I had to watch the episode five times in a row in order to move on. While I was watching it I was writing about the episode at the same time, so I should have been watching it as an outsider. Instead, I was sucked in to the setting and I was affected along with Asuka... Concerning the scene itself, GAINAX really outdid themselves. The symbols, the sounds, the colors, and the fantastic voice acting added up and really fleshed out Asuka's character in a meaningful way. In English the whole scene sounds forced and fake, though.
